questions.q: In which present-day Indian state are the Badami Cave Temples located?
questions.explanation: Badami is a town in the Bagalkot district of northern Karnataka.

questions.q: How were the Badami caves created?
options: Built up with brick and lime mortar
Carved out of solid sandstone cliffs
questions.explanation: The temples are rock-cut, hewn directly out of the red sandstone cliffs at Badami.

questions.q: Which royal dynasty created the Badami cave temples?
questions.explanation: Badami was the capital of the early (Badami) Chalukyas, who excavated the caves.

questions.q: From what type of rock are the Badami caves carved?
questions.explanation: The caves are cut from the distinctive red sandstone cliffs that surround the town.

questions.q: In roughly which century were the main Badami caves excavated?
questions.explanation: The cave temples date primarily to the 6th century CE under the early Chalukyas.

questions.q: Beside which water body do the Badami caves overlook?
questions.explanation: The caves are carved into cliffs rising above the man-made Agastya Lake.

questions.q: How many principal rock-cut cave temples form the famous Badami group?
questions.explanation: The well-known complex consists of four numbered cave temples cut into the cliff.

questions.q: The Badami caves are best known as examples of which kind of architecture?
questions.explanation: Badami is celebrated as an early masterpiece of Indian rock-cut cave temple architecture.

questions.q: Cave 4 at Badami is dedicated to which religion?
questions.explanation: Cave 4 is a Jain cave, showing Badami's religious diversity alongside Hindu caves.

questions.q: Badami was the early Chalukyas' what?
questions.explanation: Badami, then called Vatapi, served as the capital of the early Chalukya kingdom.

questions.q: By what ancient name was Badami known when it was the Chalukya capital?
questions.explanation: The town was historically called Vatapi, the capital of the Badami Chalukyas.

questions.q: Three of the four Badami caves are Hindu; to which deity are caves 2 and 3 mainly dedicated?
questions.explanation: Caves 2 and 3 are devoted to Vishnu, while Cave 1 is largely dedicated to Shiva.

questions.q: Cave 1 at Badami is famous for a large relief of Shiva in which form?
questions.explanation: Cave 1 contains a striking 18-armed Nataraja relief depicting Shiva dancing.

questions.q: Which early Chalukya king is associated with the dedicatory inscription in Cave 3 (dated 578 CE)?
questions.explanation: Cave 3 carries an inscription of the Chalukya ruler Mangalesha dated to 578 CE.

questions.q: Cave 3, the largest at Badami, is particularly admired for what feature?
options: Carved ceiling panels and large Vishnu sculptures
questions.explanation: Cave 3 is renowned for its rich ceiling panels and monumental Vishnu reliefs.

questions.q: Badami forms part of a famous cluster of Chalukyan sites; which is NOT one of its neighbours?
questions.explanation: Aihole, Pattadakal and Mahakuta are nearby Chalukyan sites; Hampi is the later Vijayanagara capital.

questions.q: Which seated Vishnu image is a celebrated sculpture in Cave 3 at Badami?
options: Vishnu seated on the coils of the serpent Ananta (Shesha)
questions.explanation: Cave 3 features a famous relief of Vishnu seated upon the coils of the serpent Ananta.

questions.q: Architecturally, each Badami cave generally consists of which sequence of spaces?
options: Verandah, columned hall (mandapa) and an inner sanctum
questions.explanation: Each cave typically has a pillared verandah, a hall, and a small sanctum carved into the rock.

questions.q: Which fierce Vishnu avatar appears as a large relief in Cave 3 at Badami?
questions.explanation: Cave 3 includes a powerful relief of Narasimha, Vishnu's man-lion incarnation.

questions.q: The Badami caves overlook a lake flanked by what other historic structures?
options: The Bhutanatha temple group and a hilltop fort
questions.explanation: Across Agastya Lake stand the Bhutanatha temples, with old forts crowning the surrounding hills.

questions.q: Which Chalukya king is generally credited with founding the capital at Vatapi (Badami) in the 6th century?
questions.explanation: Pulakeshin I established Vatapi as the Chalukya capital in the mid-6th century CE.

questions.q: Which agency is primarily responsible for maintaining the Badami cave temples today?
questions.explanation: The caves are protected monuments under the care of the Archaeological Survey of India.

questions.q: The Cave 3 inscription dated 578 CE (Saka 500) is significant for being one of the earliest dated records of what dynasty?
questions.explanation: Mangalesha's 578 CE inscription is a key early dated record of the Badami Chalukyas.

questions.q: Besides the four main caves, a later Vaishnava cave was discovered at Badami in 2015; how is it usually counted?
questions.explanation: A fifth, Vaishnava cave reported in 2015 is sometimes referred to as Cave 5 at Badami.

questions.q: In 642 CE Vatapi (Badami) was famously sacked by which rival dynasty?
questions.explanation: The Pallava king Narasimhavarman I captured and sacked Vatapi around 642 CE.

questions.q: Cave 1 at Badami includes an early depiction of which composite Shaiva form?
questions.explanation: Cave 1 contains a notable Harihara image fusing Shiva and Vishnu in one figure.

questions.q: The Badami Chalukya rival who sacked Vatapi adopted which victory title afterward?
questions.explanation: Narasimhavarman I took the title Vatapikonda, meaning conqueror of Vatapi (Badami).

questions.q: Which Chalukya ruler later decisively defeated the northern emperor Harshavardhana, marking the dynasty's peak after Badami's founding?
questions.explanation: Pulakeshin II of the Badami Chalukyas famously checked Harshavardhana's southward advance.

questions.q: The Jain cave (Cave 4) at Badami prominently features sculptures of which figures?
options: Tirthankaras such as Parshvanatha and Mahavira
questions.explanation: Cave 4 depicts Jain Tirthankaras including Parshvanatha and Mahavira, plus Bahubali.

questions.q: Badami's monuments are studied with nearby Aihole and Pattadakal because together they document what?
options: The evolution of early Chalukyan temple architecture
questions.explanation: Badami, Aihole and Pattadakal together trace the development of early Chalukyan temple architecture.
